etymology.description: semantic 彡(彡) (shān, "bristle") + phonetic 攸(攸) (yōu) -> repair
etymology.interpretation: The character 修(xiū) ("repair") combines the semantic 彡(彡) (shān, "bristle"), which suggests the concept of grooming or refining, with the phonetic 攸(攸) (yōu), contributing the sound. The combination implies bringing something back to an ideal state, hence 'repair'.

examples.chinese: 我需要修我的手机(我需要修我的手機)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ xūyào xiū wǒ de shǒujī.
examples.english: I need to repair my phone.

examples.chinese: 他会修所有的电器(他會修所有的電器)。
examples.pinyin: Tā huì xiū suǒyǒu de diànqì.
examples.english: He can repair all the appliances.

examples.chinese: 如果窗户坏了(如果窗戶壞了)，你会修吗(你會修嗎)？
examples.pinyin: Rúguǒ chuānghù huài le, nǐ huì xiū ma?
examples.english: If the window breaks, can you repair it?
